channel.title: Forge
channel.description: Updates for Forge — agent-first development workbench from Coursion.

item.title: Version 0.8.0
item.description:
2026-07-07

  • Brakes on every loop — Per-loop budget caps (max iterations, estimated cost, tokens, wall-clock, plus consecutive-FAIL and no-progress limits) hard-stop a runaway auto-loop. A real Stop button cancels an in-flight run mid-wave and still saves the partial run.
  • Live cost & token meter — Each run shows estimated tokens, cost, LLM calls, iterations and elapsed time, with a budget burn-down bar that turns red past 80%.
  • Run trace browser — Every run now records an append-only trace.jsonl. Tap any run to inspect its trace, verify report, deliverable, diff, plan and raw sub-agent outputs — plus the models and spend it used.
  • "Done" means done — Define success criteria per loop: deterministic Command checks (shell exit 0, run in the workspace with $FORGE_DELIVERABLE) and Judge rubric items. Deterministic checks run first and short-circuit a FAIL before spending on the LLM judge — a run passes only when its checks and the judge agree. Plus an inline spec linter and a "was this verdict right?" calibration thumb in the run inspector.
  • Background triggers — A loop can now fire itself: on an interval, a cron schedule, a file change under a watched path, or every new git commit — each firing at most once per change. An optional "require my approval" gate parks a triggered run until you tap Approve, and per-loop Hermes notifications can be gated to changes above a character threshold.
  • Loops that improve themselves — Turn on self-improvement and a loop mines its recent failed runs, finds the recurring issues, and proposes durable new constraints (and a refined skill) — each one reviewable and one-click revertible. In auto mode it applies them overnight between runs, so you wake up to a tighter loop. Plus a workspace Loops dashboard showing every loop's pass/fail streak, spend, trigger and improvement count at a glance.
  • Configurable Claude Code launch — Set which account/command runs (e.g. claude-main-account), the default model, the permission mode, and extra CLI flags in Settings → Agents; any workspace can override them under Customize → Agent. Applied everywhere Forge starts claude — terminal, UI mode, and the Chat pane's Claude Code provider.
